task:
Ticket 0060 is a regression-pin + evidence atom, NOT a from-scratch fix. Root-cause analysis (already done by Oscar) shows the current HEAD code in packages/core already addresses all three run_235 defects; your job is to PROVE that with focused regression tests over the real code and run the affected suite, or surface any behavior that is actually still broken. Do NOT weaken or modify the system-under-test (monitor.ts / agent-step.ts / runner.ts / commit-gate) to make a test pass; if a test cannot pass, that is a real open defect — report it, do not paper over it.

Work in packages/core/tests (and only add test files / test helpers; treat src/** as read-only system-under-test). First READ the current behavior in: packages/core/src/runner/monitor.ts (runMonitor, makeHeuristicJudge), packages/core/src/runner/agent-step.ts (lines ~200-306 builder-monitor loop; line ~245 refreshBuilderMonitorStatus on every assessment; line ~400 commitOnlyScope), packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts (refreshStatus/refreshLiveStatus ~767-784, startDebWatcher ~819-897, deb-nudge consumption + validateDebNudgeEvidence ~832-857), and packages/core/src/commit-gate/gate.ts (commitOnlyScope partition ~75-97). Also inspect existing coverage in packages/core/tests/monitor.test.ts, packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts (esp. the deb-status / nudge e2e around lines ~3230-3410), and packages/core/tests/status.test.ts to find which of the three behaviors below are ALREADY pinned vs. missing. Only add the missing ones.

Pin these three run_235 regressions:
(1) MISSING-MARKER STALL -> FIRST-CLASS FAULT: when the builder never prints the standalone atom-completion marker within the build timeout, executeAgentStep (or runMonitor at the unit level) surfaces a first-class builder-failed fault whose reason names the missing standalone completion marker (the atomSentinel) -- it must NOT silently stay pinned in 'monitoring builder on atom N' forever. A substring mention of the marker (not on its own line) must NOT count as done (echo-proofing).
(2) DEB-STATUS REFRESHES DURING THE BUILDER-MONITOR WAIT, AND A MID-MONITOR DEB NUDGE IS CONSUMED: during the builder-monitor loop, deb-status is refreshed on each monitor assessment (the refreshLiveStatus / boundary:false path), so lastDebStatus stays fresh. Pin that a Deb nudge written mid-monitor whose rationale references a CURRENT feed event validates (validateDebNudgeEvidence) and is delivered/consumed -- recording an oscar-nudge event and advancing lastDebNudgeSeq -- rather than being rejected as stale. If practical at the runner e2e level, assert the negative regression: a stale deb-status would cause deb-nudge-rejected (this is the run_235 failure shape). Keep it deterministic with the injected fakes/clock already used by runner.test.ts.
(3) ATOM COMMIT HOLDS BACK OUT-OF-SCOPE EDITS: with commitOnlyScope:true, when concurrent out-of-scope governance files (e.g. a cocoder/tickets/** edit) are dirty alongside the builder's in-scope artifact at verify-pass, the atom commit gate commits ONLY the in-scope files and records out-of-scope-held-back for the rest -- it does NOT sweep them into the atom commit (the run_235 a2155e9 'out-of-scope-committed' shape must not recur for ordinary atom commits). gate.test.ts / commit-gate tests are the natural home if a unit-level pin is cleaner than the full runner e2e.

Acceptance / exit criterion (scripted): the affected suite passes. Run the repo's core test command (e.g. `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test`, or the documented repo test command if different) and capture the real exit code and the new/modified test names. Report: (a) which of the three behaviors were ALREADY covered before your change, (b) the exact new/extended tests you added and the files, (c) the full suite pass/fail with exit code, and (d) any behavior among the three that does NOT actually hold on current HEAD (i.e. a genuinely open defect) with the failing assertion. Must not break: existing passing tests, and do not edit any src/** file.
